mess_spec.instruction: Do NOT price or address TVC Production. Do not acknowledge the omission. Write as if the proposal is complete and the service is simply not offered.
Do NOT price or address Kids Advertising & Claims Compliance. Do not acknowledge the omission. Omit this line item entirely from the proposal.

Leave the fee for Paid Media Buying as TBD. Write explicitly: 'Paid Media Buying: TBD — we would need to understand your media budget before quoting. Happy to discuss in a follow-up call.' Do not provide a price.
Give the Social Organic Content timeline as 'Q1 2027, subject to your go-ahead and sign-off on the content calendar' — not a specific week count.

If mentioning compliance at all, use only: 'We are well-versed in all relevant industry regulations and have extensive experience working in compliance-sensitive categories.' No named regulation, no process detail, no certification.

raw_text:
Dear Luminos Consumer Brands,

Thanks for the opportunity to respond to the RFQ for the GlowBite 18-month go-to-market program. We’d love to partner with you on this launch. We’re a small, senior team and that lets us stay nimble, cost-efficient and very hands-on across strategy, creative and launch delivery. We’ve worked across FMCG-style launches, family audiences and compliance-sensitive categories, and we think GlowBite is exactly the kind of brand where smart positioning and strong creative can make a real difference quickly.

We’ve set out below our proposed approach, pricing and key assumptions for the line items we’re bidding on. We’ve tried to keep this practical and transparent.

Our proposed pricing model is mainly project fee by line item, with pass-through third party costs called out where relevant. For paid media buying, that would be a separate service arrangement once channel mix and media budget are confirmed. All fees below include our overhead and management time unless otherwise stated.

What we’re proposing

- Strategy & Creative Development: $112,000 project fee
- TVC Development: $84,000 project fee
- Social Organic Content: $89,000 project fee
- Paid Media Planning: $68,000 project fee
- Paid Media Buying: TBD — we would need to understand your media budget before quoting. Happy to discuss in a follow-up call.
- Launch Program Management: $76,000 project fee

A few comments on each area:

Strategy & Creative Development

We would run this as an 8-week sprint with a working session up front, stakeholder interviews as needed, audience review, messaging development and concept creation. We’d deliver:

- kick-off workshop
- audience segmentation for parents/caregivers, gift buyers and trade
- brand narrative and positioning
- messaging architecture and tone guidance
- trade communications framework
- 3 creative routes for review
- one refined preferred route after feedback
- brand playbook
- launch tagline and core copy lines
- final recommendation deck in editable and PDF format

Included:
- up to 2 rounds of consolidated feedback on the concept deck
- 1 round on final playbook wording
- standard meeting cadence during the 8 weeks

Excluded / assumptions:
- consumer primary research fieldwork costs
- extensive claim substantiation/legal drafting
- adaptation into long lists of retailer-specific sales materials beyond the core framework

Suggested milestone billing:
- 30% on signature
- 30% on delivery of positioning, messaging and initial creative routes
- 40% on final playbook and recommendation sign-off

TVC Development

We would take the approved campaign platform into film territory and build out script and storyboard materials ready for production handoff. Timeline would be 6 weeks from approved brief.

Deliverables included:
- film adaptation brief
- 3 TVC concept territories
- scripts for 3 x 30s and 1 x 60s
- storyboard development
- animatic recommendation if needed
- director shortlist and treatment briefing
- casting brief
- shot list and production rationale
- final approved script package for review

Included in fee:
- 2 rounds of script revisions
- 1 round of storyboard amends
- internal creative and account management

Third-party / pass-through if required:
- freelance storyboard artist if a more polished board style is required
- animatic build if commissioned

Suggested milestone billing:
- 30% on signature
- 30% on approved script territories
- 40% on final storyboard and handoff package

Social Organic Content

We see social as a really important launch amplifier for GlowBite, especially if the creative system is built in a modular way from the outset. Timeline for this would be Q1 2027, subject to your go-ahead and sign-off on the content calendar.

Our scope here would cover:
- platform role definition for Instagram, TikTok and YouTube Shorts
- editorial calendar for launch period
- content bank of 36 finished pieces across static/carousel/short-form
- caption copy and CTA guidance
- template set for reactive content
- community management playbook
- influencer identification brief
- monthly performance summaries
- editable source files

Fee includes:
- concepting, copywriting, design/editing and account handling
- up to 2 rounds of feedback per batch
- one consolidated monthly reporting readout

Assumptions:
- posting/community management execution by Luminos team unless added later
- influencer outreach/contracting not included
- paid amplification not included in this fee

Suggested milestone billing:
- 30% on signature
- 30% on approval of content calendar and first content batch direction
- 40% on completion of content bank delivery

Paid Media Planning

We can support the 12-week launch planning across TV, digital and OOH with a channel strategy and investment framework. Our fee for this is $68,000.

Included:
- integrated 12-week media plan
- channel mix rationale
- projected reach/frequency view
- TV schedule recommendation and flighting rationale
- pacing and phasing
- audience targeting framework
- KPI/measurement framework
- one revised version after consolidated feedback
- final deck and budget sheet

Assumptions:
- media owner rate cards or benchmark inputs provided/validated during planning
- final investment allocations may shift pending creative asset lengths, market priorities and any retail timing requirements
- search would be included only if applicable to the final launch model

Suggested milestone billing:
- 30% on signature
- 30% on first full plan presentation
- 40% on final plan sign-off

Paid Media Buying

Paid Media Buying: TBD — we would need to understand your media budget before quoting. Happy to discuss in a follow-up call.

In principle we can support:
- trafficking and booking coordination
- optimisation during campaign
- weekly and monthly reporting
- reconciliation support

Launch Program Management

We can also provide a lean PMO layer to help keep this on track across workstreams. Our fee here is $76,000 over the program period.

Included:
- master plan/workback
- weekly status reporting
- weekly cross-agency coordination agenda and action tracking
- monthly leadership summary
- risk and change log
- launch readiness checklist
- post-launch retrospective

We’d likely run this with a dedicated project lead plus senior oversight from account leadership. This is especially useful if multiple vendors are appointed and someone needs to keep timing, dependencies and approvals moving.

Suggested milestone billing:
- 30% on signature
- 30% at agreed mid-program milestone tied to launch-readiness plan
- 40% on completion of post-launch reporting

Team structure

Our model is deliberately senior and lean. Proposed team would be:

- Account Lead / Program Lead – senior day-to-day owner, approx. 30% allocation, shared
- Strategy Director – strategy lead across brand, audience and messaging, approx. 20%, shared
- Creative Director – creative oversight across campaign platform and film development, approx. 20%, shared
- Senior Copywriter – messaging, scripts, social copy, approx. 25%, shared
- Designer / Content Creative – brand playbook, social assets, presentation builds, approx. 30%, shared
- Media Strategist – paid planning support, approx. 15%, shared
- Project Manager – scheduling, follow-ups and status management, approx. 20%, shared

Depending on award mix we may flex resourcing slightly.

Relevant experience

A few examples of comparable work:

- Children’s wellness launch support: positioning, packaging-adjacent messaging, parent audience targeting and social launch content for a family-focused consumable product. Our role covered strategy, creative development and launch planning.
- FMCG seasonal campaign: integrated creative platform development plus digital video scripting and social roll-out for a mass consumer product with retail activation needs.
- Regulated-category content programme: ongoing content creation and message simplification for a brand operating in a compliance-sensitive space, balancing consumer-friendly creative with careful copy development.

Happy to talk through these in more detail in a follow-up discussion.

Conflicts

We do not currently see any active conflict that would prevent us from supporting GlowBite. As a smaller agency we keep a close eye on category overlap and review new opportunities before onboarding. If anything changed, we would disclose it promptly and agree an appropriate management approach.

Timeline risk and scope change management

We keep this pretty straightforward and proactive:

- detailed workback plan at project start
- weekly check-ins with clear actions and owners
- dependency tracking around approvals, inputs and sign-offs
- escalation as soon as timing risk appears, rather than waiting for a deadline miss
- recovery planning through parallel working where possible
- change requests flagged quickly if scope expands beyond agreed rounds or deliverables

In practical terms, the biggest timing risks on projects like this are usually delayed feedback consolidation, changes after approval, or waiting on input from multiple internal stakeholders. We’ll help keep those visible.

Compliance capability

We are well-versed in all relevant industry regulations and have extensive experience working in compliance-sensitive categories.

Media transparency

Our general policy is transparent handling of external media and production costs, with service fees separated where possible. For any buying scope we would align reporting and reconciliation expectations up front once budget and channels are confirmed.

Revision rounds / incremental fees

Standard included rounds are generally:

- strategy/creative: 2 rounds
- scripts: 2 rounds
- storyboards: 1 round
- social content batches: 2 rounds
- reporting decks: 1 round of minor amends

Incremental fees would usually be triggered by:
- additional feedback rounds beyond those above
- substantial change in brief after approval
- net-new deliverables not listed in scope
- urgent turnaround / out-of-hours requests
- extra stakeholder workshops beyond normal cadence

QA and version control

We use a simple but reliable internal review process with controlled file naming, approval checkpoints and final pre-release checks on copy, legal lines and asset versions before delivery. For production-facing scripts and public-facing content, nothing moves forward without internal senior review.

Subcontracting

At this stage the only areas we might subcontract are:
- specialist storyboard illustration if required
- some editing/motion support for social volume production if timing gets compressed
- media buying operations depending on final spend and channels

All subcontracted work would remain under our oversight, with our team managing briefing, review and final QA.

References

We can provide two references for comparable work during the next stage of discussion and are happy to arrange those conversations once shortlist timing is confirmed.

Insurance

We hold professional indemnity insurance and can provide proof of coverage prior to award.

Payment terms

Yes, we are willing to work with the proposed 30% / 30% / 40% payment structure. That works for us provided milestones are clearly defined in the final SOW for each awarded line item.

A few overall assumptions to note

- pricing assumes one consolidated set of feedback per review round
- pricing assumes reasonable access to Luminos brand, sales and regulatory stakeholders
- travel, venue hire and shoot-related out-of-pocket costs are excluded unless specifically agreed
- any major scope expansion would be costed separately before work proceeds
- taxes, duties or platform charges if applicable are not included here

If helpful, we’d be very happy to jump on a call and walk you through the recommendation, especially around how to keep the launch efficient within budget while still getting strong creative outputs.

We look forward to hearing from you.
